steps.title: Extract the system from the graph
steps.content: The slanted line passes through (0, 3) with slope $-\frac{1}{2}$, giving $x + 2y = 6$. The horizontal line is $y = 2$. These two equations form the original system.

steps.title: Solve the original system
steps.content:
Substitute $y = 2$ into $x + 2y = 6$:
$$x + 2(2) = 6$$
$$x = 2$$
The original system has solution $(2, 2)$.

steps.title: Check if the solution satisfies the new equation
steps.content:
Test $(2, 2)$ in $x + 4y = -16$:
$$2 + 4(2) = 2 + 8 = 10$$
Since $10 \neq -16$, the point $(2, 2)$ does not lie on the third line. Therefore, no point satisfies all three equations. That matches choice A — A is correct.
